unitTitle: Unit 19: Questions / Question tags / Indirect questions

content.subtitle: Form with 'be' as a main verb
items: Am/Was I on time?
Are/Were you/we/they tired?
Is/Was he/she/it cold?

content.subtitle: Form with 'be' as an auxiliary verb
items: Am/Was I interrupting you?
Are/Were you/we/they going on a picnic?
Is/Was he/she/it working?

content.subtitle: Form with 'have' as an auxiliary verb
items: Have/Had I/you/we/they got any money?
Has/Had he/she/it finished?

content.subtitle: Form with 'have' as a main verb and with all other verbs
items: Do/Did I/you/we/they have enough time?
Does/Did he/she/it need anything?

content.subtitle: Form with modals
items: Should I wait?
Could you help me?
Will she be here soon?
Might they be lost?

content.subtitle: Form with question words
items: Who is taking the rubbish out?
Whose book is this?
To whom did you speak?
What is the weather like?
Which do you want?
Where did you go on holiday?
When is Terry starting work?
Why did they leave?
How do you spell 'environment'?

content.subtitle: Subject vs. object questions
content.description: With 'who' and 'what', whether we use 'do' or not depends on whether the question word refers to the subject or object of the verb.
examples: Subject: Who saw you? (= Someone saw you. Who?)
Object: Who did you see? (= You saw someone. Who?)

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: After 'do' or 'does', we use the bare infinitive: Did you go to the talk on the environment?
The verb 'mean' forms questions just like other main verbs: What does 'environmental' mean?

content.subtitle: Form with 'be' as a main verb
items: You are Canadian, aren't you?
She is beautiful, isn't she?

content.subtitle: Form with auxiliary verbs and modals
items: You haven't lost my CD, have you?
We are having the lesson early tomorrow, aren't we?
People should recycle things, shouldn't they?
There will be lots of people there, won't there?

content.subtitle: Form with 'have' as a main verb
items: Tom has a lovely voice, hasn't/doesn't he?

content.subtitle: Form with other verbs
items: You play the guitar, don't you?
Frank lives in Germany now, doesn't he?
Your friends really enjoyed themselves at the party, didn't they?

content.subtitle: Form with 'Let's'
items: Let's get a DVD tonight, shall we?

content.subtitle: Form with imperatives
items: Pass me that book, will/would/could you?
Don't forget tonight, will you?

content.subtitle: Use
items: To ask someone to agree with us (falling intonation): It's really hot, isn't it?
To check whether something is true (rising intonation): You're Spanish, aren't you?

content.subtitle: Special cases
items: With 'I am', we use 'aren't?' as the question tag: I'm the best student in the class, aren't I?
With 'I am not', we use 'am I?': I'm not very tall, am I?
With everyone, no one and someone, we use question tags with a plural verb and 'they': Everyone's going to be there, aren't they?
In sentences with a negative word like no, little, never, nobody, no one, hardly, etc, we use a positive question tag: You have got no manners, have you?
In sentences where the subject is 'there', we repeat 'there' in the question tag: There's no point calling Tim now, is there?

content.subtitle: American English note
content.description: In American English, a question tag with 'do' can be used after a sentence with 'have got'. This is not usually done in British English.
examples: US: They've got a lot of money, don't they?
UK: They've got a lot of money, haven't they?

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: question word + subject + verb (statement word order)

content.subtitle: Use
content.description: We use indirect questions when we want to ask questions politely.
examples: Could you tell me what time it is?
Could you let me know when it starts?
Do you know who that woman is?
I wonder if you could tell me how much this costs.
I wonder if you know what the starting salary is.
I would like to know what your company is going to do about it.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: We do not use question word order in the second part of the sentence: ✓ I would like to know when the next train to London leaves.
Some indirect questions need a question mark at the end: Could you tell me where the library is?
If a direct question is a 'yes/no' question, the equivalent indirect question uses 'if' or 'whether': I wonder if/whether you have read this book.

items.sentence: Oh, I'm sorry! ...... disturbing you?
Did Dorothy ...... you about the meeting on Friday?
...... hot when you were in Spain?
Tony, ...... got time to call Mrs Roberts?
When your boss arrived, ...... finished the report?
I know Jane's pretty, but ...... seen someone spend so much time in front of the mirror?
It's raining, so ...... cancel the concert?
...... pass me the salt, please?
Sir, ...... repeat what the homework is, please?
...... the couple you met in France last year staying at the same hotel?
I know how to pronounce 'controversy', but what ......?
It's a great idea, but ...... it will work?

sections.title: B: Write questions
sections.instructions: Write questions using the words given.

items.sentence: you / wash / your hair / when I rang?
items.answer: Were you washing your hair when I rang?

items.sentence: Julie / give / you / her e-mail address / yesterday?
items.answer: Did Julie give you her e-mail address yesterday?

items.sentence: you / always / have / lunch / this late?
items.answer: Do you always have lunch this late?

items.sentence: Jack and Tom / come / to the party / tonight?
items.answer: Are Jack and Tom coming to the party tonight?

items.sentence: you / can / give / me / a hand / later?
items.answer: Can you give me a hand later?

items.sentence: how / you / spell / your name?
items.answer: How do you spell your name?

items.sentence: why / the government / can't / do / something / about the situation?
items.answer: Why can't the government do something about the situation?

items.sentence: where / you / go / for your honeymoon / last year?
items.answer: Where did you go for your honeymoon last year?

items.sentence: what / your house / look like / when / it is finished?
items.answer: What will your house look like when it is finished?

items.sentence: which / flavour of ice cream / your favourite / be?
items.answer: Which flavour of ice cream is your favourite?

sections.title: C: Question words
sections.instructions: Write a question word in each gap.

items.sentence: ...... car is that? Is it yours?
...... do you usually go on holiday?
...... is the woman in the red dress?
...... didn't you come to the party?
...... do you switch this thing on?
...... did you buy at the supermarket?
...... one do you want? The red one or the blue one?
...... is the nearest post office?

sections.title: D: Subject vs. object questions
sections.instructions: Rewrite the sentences to make them into questions. Begin with the word in brackets.

items.sentence:
I asked my mum.
**who** ......

I saw my best friend.
**who** ......

My dad gave me a CD.
**who** ......

I thought you were in Paris.
**what** ......
items.answer: What made you think I was in Paris?

items.sentence:
Miss Jones taught us history.
**who** ......

I borrowed a video from Jamie.
**who** ......
items.answer: Who did you borrow a video from?

items.sentence:
My uncle brought me a present from Canada.
**who** ......
items.answer: Who brought you a present from Canada?

items.sentence:
George accused me of stealing his pencil.
**who** ......
items.answer: Who did George accuse of stealing his pencil?

items.sentence:
I admire my mum more than anyone else.
**who** ......
items.answer: Who do you admire more than anyone else?

items.sentence:
I told Dave the good news.
**who** ......
items.answer: Who told Dave the good news?

sections.title: E: Question words (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: (1) ...... you know what the time is?
(2) ...... didn't you tell me you were going out?
What (3) ...... I do to make you happy?
What (4) ...... this button do?
(5) ...... you tell me the way to the station, please?
(6) ...... did you buy that hat? In Paris?
(7) ...... do you know my name?
(8) ...... are you laughing at?
You've sent that letter I gave you, **D** ...haven't you?
You catch the bus to school, **A** ...don't you?
You won't tell anyone about this, **C** ...will you?
You're a friend of Charlie's, **H** ...aren't you?
You were living in Hong Kong then, **G** ...weren't you?
You never work more than you have to, **E** ...do you?
You made no effort to make friends with Darren, **F** ...did you?
You got Jimmy a cap for his birthday, **B** ...didn't you?
Get me some chewing gum when you go to the shop, ...... you?
Let's watch that new DVD you bought today, ...... we?
There's not really much point waiting, ...... there?
Tonia will put us up for the weekend, ...... she?
Nobody seems to like Jessica, ...... they?
I'm not making much sense now, ...... I?
Let's go because it's getting late, ...... it?
If you borrow my coat, don't get it dirty, ...... you?
Bill should be here by now, ...... he?
I'm making you feel uncomfortable, ...... I?
Someone left the door open, ...... they?
Nobody knows about this, ...... they?
I wonder if you could tell me what time does the plane from Frankfurt arrive?
items.answer: I wonder if you could tell me what time the plane from Frankfurt arrives.

items.sentence: Could you let me know when would you like me to come for an interview.
items.answer: Could you let me know when you would like me to come for an interview?

items.sentence: I wonder if you know what bus should I catch for the town centre.
items.answer: I wonder if you know which bus I should catch for the town centre.

items.sentence: Do you think you could tell me how do you work this ticket machine?
items.answer: Do you think you could tell me how you work this ticket machine?

items.sentence: I wonder you have seen George?
items.answer: I wonder if you have seen George./I wonder whether you have seen George.

items.sentence: I would like to know do you have any double rooms?
items.answer: I would like to know if you have any double rooms./I would like to know whether you have any double rooms.

items.sentence: Can you tell me what were you doing in my office?
items.answer: Can you tell me what you were doing in my office?

items.sentence: Do you know where is this address?
items.answer: Do you know where this address is?

sections.title: I: Key word transformation (Indirect questions)
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
When does Tina get back from Berlin?
**know** Do ...... back from Berlin?

What time does the film start tonight?
**starts** Could you tell me ...... tonight?
items.answer: what time the film starts/when the film starts

items.sentence:
Is service included in the price?
**know** I would like to ...... in the price.
items.answer: know if service is included/know whether service is included

items.sentence:
What is the salary?
**let** Could you ...... the salary is?

Have you been to Brussels before?
**wonder** I ...... to Brussels before.
items.answer: wonder if you have been/wonder whether you have been

items.sentence:
Did Gail pass her exam?
**passed** Do you know ...... her exam?

I wonder if you know where Mary went after the party last night.
**go** Where ...... after the party last night?

I would like to know how many days holiday we get each year.
**given** How many days holiday ...... each year?

sections.title: J: Error correction (extra words)
sections.instructions: There is one extra word in each line. Click the extra word.

items.sentence: Do you have much free time these days or are you be quite busy?
I would like to know it when I can expect my order to be delivered.
Do you think whether you could possibly let me know how soon you will have the work finished?
I wonder if you know who it is responsible for cleaning the building.
Tell Roger who did you saw when you were at the police station the other day.
Did Dad mention who he sold him the car to?
You shouldn't leave your homework to the very last minute if you want to get a good mark, should not you?
I wonder it if you know where I can buy something to eat.
